name: Five Children Went Upstairs. The Fire Left No Bones.

video.title: Five Children Went Upstairs. The Fire Left No Bones.
video.global_style_suffix: dark carnival harlequin-noir illustration, bold flat vector shapes with thick confident ink outlines, harlequin diamond motifs and ornate vintage sideshow-poster borders, dramatic single-source spotlight cutting deep shadow, gaslight-gold and blood-crimson and bone-white on near-black aubergine, subtle velvet texture, macabre elegance, high contrast, clean uncluttered composition

direction_card.hook_style: open with a clock — timestamps structure the acts
direction_card.ending: the clock stops where the record stops

video.global_negative_prompt: photorealistic, 3d render, gore, blood splatter, realistic human faces, modern clothing, readable text, gibberish letters, cluttered, washed out, low contrast, neon, watermark

voice.speaker: Ryan
voice.instruct: Speak calmly and quietly, almost flat, like a tired keeper walking one guest through the exhibits after closing. Slow, even pace, small natural pauses, soft breaths between sentences; tiny hesitations and worn edges are welcome. Never theatrical, never excited. Let the facts do the frightening.

settings.music_vibe: calm dark ambient, faraway broken music box, low drones, slow airy pads, unhurried

scenes.narration: Five children went upstairs on Christmas Eve. They never came down.
scenes.image_prompt: a dark wooden farmhouse staircase at night, five small shadowed children climbing up, warm lamplight below, long shadows

scenes.narration: By morning, their house was a hole full of smoking ash.
scenes.image_prompt: a smoking rectangular foundation pit where a farmhouse stood, ash and embers, snow-dusted ground, cold dawn, deserted

scenes.narration: The fire took forty-five minutes to eat the whole thing.
scenes.image_prompt: a two-story wooden farmhouse fully engulfed in fire at night, flames through every window, sparks rising

scenes.narration: Searchers raked the ashes for bones. They found nothing.
scenes.image_prompt: a man raking through knee-deep grey ash inside a burned foundation, morning light, empty hands

scenes.narration: No skulls. No teeth. Nothing, from five children.
scenes.image_prompt: fine grey ash falling through a wooden sifting screen held by two hands, nothing caught in the mesh

scenes.narration: The phone line had not burned. Someone had cut it.
scenes.image_prompt: a severed telephone wire hanging from a wooden pole against the night sky, clean-cut end frayed with copper

scenes.narration: The ladder that always leaned on the house was gone.
scenes.image_prompt: the empty clapboard wall of a farmhouse at night, a pale ladder-shaped outline in the frost where one stood

scenes.narration: Eighty years later, nobody has explained that night.
scenes.image_prompt: a roadside billboard weathered by eighty years, five faded oval child portraits, empty mountain road at dusk, deserted

scenes.narration: This is the exhibit Fayetteville never closed. The Sodder children.
scenes.image_prompt: a dark carnival exhibit pedestal holding one framed photograph of five children, single spotlight, red curtain behind

scenes.narration: Rewind to ten p.m., December 24th, 1945.
scenes.image_prompt: a wooden mantel clock showing ten o'clock, pine garland and one candle beside it, warm low light, nobody around

scenes.narration: Fayetteville, West Virginia. Coal country, deep in the mountains.
scenes.image_prompt: wide establishing shot of a small 1940s Appalachian coal town in snowy mountains at night, few golden windows, deserted

scenes.narration: George Sodder had built this life from nothing.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder standing proud in front of his two-story farmhouse, thumbs in suspenders, snow falling

scenes.narration: He left Sardinia at thirteen, alone, and never said why.
scenes.image_prompt: a small boy with one suitcase standing alone at a ship's rail, grey ocean, gulls, cold light

scenes.narration: Whatever George ran from, he never spoke of Italy again.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder's face half in shadow beside a dark window, closed guarded expression, lamplight edge

scenes.narration: Now his coal trucks fed ten children.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder beside a loaded 1940s flatbed coal truck, farmhouse behind him, proud stance

scenes.narration: That night, nine of them were home.
scenes.image_prompt: the farmhouse at night with warm glowing windows, small silhouettes of children moving inside, snow falling

scenes.narration: The tenth, Joe, was away in the Army.
scenes.image_prompt: a framed Army photograph of a young soldier in uniform on a mantel beside a burning candle, nobody around

scenes.narration: Five-year-old Betty had new toys from the dime store.
scenes.image_prompt: a five-year-old girl in a nightgown playing with a new tin wind-up toy under a small Christmas tree, oil-lamp glow

scenes.narration: Her big sister Marion had spent her whole paycheck on them.
scenes.image_prompt: a smiling teenage girl in a cardigan arranging the toys her paycheck bought, wrapping paper scraps, candlelight

scenes.narration: The five youngest begged to stay up and play.
scenes.image_prompt: five children of different ages gathered around a lit Christmas tree in a dim farmhouse parlor, playing on the rug

scenes.narration: Their mother Jennie said yes, if the chores got done.
scenes.image_prompt: Jennie Sodder in an apron pointing gently toward the kitchen door, warm kitchen light, bread on the table

scenes.narration: So Maurice, fourteen, fed the cows. Louis, nine, shut the coop.
scenes.image_prompt: two boys in coats crossing a snowy farmyard at night, the older toward the cows in the barn, the younger toward the chicken coop

scenes.narration: Ordinary chores, on the last ordinary night.
scenes.image_prompt: the farmhouse under a huge starry night sky, one lantern crossing the yard on the last of the chores, quiet

scenes.narration: Because strange things had circled this family for months.
scenes.image_prompt: the family's lonely farmhouse seen from far across a dark snowy field, as if watched from the treeline, deserted foreground

scenes.narration: In October, an insurance salesman had come to the door.
scenes.image_prompt: a man in a long dark coat and hat standing at the farmhouse front door at dusk, briefcase in hand

scenes.narration: George turned him down. The man's face changed.
scenes.image_prompt: the salesman leaning in close with a raised finger, George Sodder's jaw set hard in the doorway

scenes.narration: Your house will go up in smoke, he warned.
scenes.image_prompt: the salesman's long shadow stretched across the porch toward the house door, a thin line of chimney smoke above, dusk

scenes.narration: Your children, he said, are going to be destroyed.
scenes.image_prompt: Jennie Sodder frozen in the kitchen, hand stopped on a dish towel, the children's laughter felt through the wall

scenes.narration: The reason he gave: George's dirty remarks about Mussolini.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder's reading table: a crumpled Italian-language newspaper beside an oil lamp, deep shadows, nobody around

scenes.narration: George was loud about hating the dictator. Fayetteville's Italians had noticed.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder arguing outside a general store with two other men, jabbing a finger at a folded newspaper

scenes.narration: Weeks later, a stranger came asking for hauling work.
scenes.image_prompt: a stranger with a tool bag peering around the back corner of the farmhouse, grey afternoon

scenes.narration: He wandered to the back and studied the fuse boxes.
scenes.image_prompt: two metal fuse boxes on a clapboard wall, a gloved hand reaching toward them, overcast light

scenes.narration: These will cause a fire someday, the stranger said.
scenes.image_prompt: the stranger's face shadowed under a hat brim, eyes fixed on the wiring, close and uneasy

scenes.narration: Odd, the power company had just passed that new wiring as safe.
scenes.image_prompt: fresh electrical wiring stapled neatly along a farmhouse beam with a small paper inspection tag wired to it, nobody around

scenes.narration: And before Christmas, the older boys noticed a parked car.
scenes.image_prompt: a dark 1940s sedan parked on a highway shoulder above the farm, exhaust curling in cold air

scenes.narration: The men inside watched the younger children walk home from school.
scenes.image_prompt: view through a car windshield of small children walking a snowy road ahead, wipers still, ominous framing

scenes.narration: Nobody thought to write down the plate.
scenes.image_prompt: the dark sedan driving away down a grey mountain road, its rear plate unreadable in the dark, red taillights shrinking

scenes.narration: Half past midnight. The phone rang.
scenes.image_prompt: a black rotary wall telephone mid-ring in a dark farmhouse hallway, moonlight through a window, nobody around

scenes.narration: Jennie answered. A woman asked for a name she didn't know.
scenes.image_prompt: Jennie Sodder in a nightgown holding the receiver, candlestick in her other hand, hallway darkness around her

scenes.narration: Glasses clinked behind the voice. Then a strange, wrong laugh.
scenes.image_prompt: extreme close-up of the telephone earpiece against Jennie Sodder's ear as the strange laugh crackles through, her eyes wide

scenes.narration: Wrong number, Jennie said, and hung up.
scenes.image_prompt: Jennie Sodder setting the receiver down slowly, staring at the telephone

scenes.narration: Walking back, she noticed the downstairs lights still burning.
scenes.image_prompt: an empty farmhouse hallway downstairs with lamplight burning from a doorway, deep shadows, nobody visible

scenes.narration: The curtains open. The front door unlocked.
scenes.image_prompt: a farmhouse front door ajar one inch, cold blue night through the gap, latch hanging unlocked

scenes.narration: Marion was asleep on the sofa. Everything else seemed normal.
scenes.image_prompt: a teenage girl asleep on a parlor sofa under a quilt, Christmas tree glowing softly beside her

scenes.narration: So Jennie went back to bed, and the clock kept moving.
scenes.image_prompt: Jennie Sodder climbing dark stairs with a candle, a clock face glowing faint in the hall below

scenes.narration: One a.m. A hard thump hit the roof.
scenes.image_prompt: the farmhouse roofline against the stars, a burst of snow sliding from one point of impact

scenes.narration: Then a rolling sound, like a rubber ball, down the shingles.
scenes.image_prompt: close-up of dark roof shingles with a round shadowed object mid-roll, moonlight, motion

scenes.narration: Then silence. Jennie half woke, listened, and drifted off.
scenes.image_prompt: Jennie Sodder's face half awake on a pillow, eyes opening toward the ceiling, candle out

scenes.narration: Half past one. She woke again, to smoke.
scenes.image_prompt: thin grey smoke curling under a bedroom door, orange light flickering through the gap

scenes.narration: George's office was burning. The stairway was next.
scenes.image_prompt: flames climbing a wooden desk and a wall of papers in a small farmhouse office, violent orange light

scenes.narration: Six people made it out into the snow.
scenes.image_prompt: a barefoot family huddled in a snowy yard at night, lit orange by their burning house, clutching each other

scenes.narration: Upstairs, five children slept behind one dark window.
scenes.image_prompt: an upstairs farmhouse window still dark above spreading firelight, flame reaching up the wall

scenes.narration: Maurice. Martha. Louis. Little Jennie. Betty.
scenes.image_prompt: five oval framed portraits of children — Maurice, Martha, Louis, little Jennie, Betty — on a papered wall, firelight flickering across them

scenes.narration: George screamed their names. The fire answered.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder mid-scream toward the burning house, embers streaming past his face, snow around his feet

scenes.narration: He ran for the ladder that always leaned against the house.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder sprinting along the farmhouse wall in firelight toward an empty stretch of clapboard

scenes.narration: Gone. On this night of all nights.
scenes.image_prompt: bare clapboard wall in firelight, a clean ladder-shaped mark in the frost — gone, nothing leaning there tonight

scenes.narration: It was found later, thrown down an embankment, seventy-five feet away.
scenes.image_prompt: a wooden ladder lying in dead brush at the bottom of a steep embankment, pale dawn, frost

scenes.narration: So George ran for his coal trucks, to climb from their roofs.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder yanking open the door of a 1940s coal truck, fire reflected in the windshield

scenes.narration: Both had run perfectly that afternoon. Now neither would start.
scenes.image_prompt: two dead coal trucks that will not start, dark hoods in a snowy yard, the house blazing huge behind them

scenes.narration: George tore his arm open smashing back in through a window.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder's silhouette punching through a ground-floor window, glass shards catching red light

scenes.narration: The water barrel had frozen solid.
scenes.image_prompt: a wooden rain barrel capped with a thick disc of ice, flames reflected in the frozen surface

scenes.narration: Marion ran through the snow to phone from the neighbors.
scenes.image_prompt: a teenage girl running barefoot through deep snow toward a lit neighboring farmhouse, night

scenes.narration: The operator never answered. Not for her. Not for anyone.
scenes.image_prompt: an unattended telephone switchboard with cords hanging dead, an empty operator's chair, one small lamp

scenes.narration: A passing driver saw the flames and tried a tavern phone.
scenes.image_prompt: a man gripping a wall telephone inside a dim roadside tavern, urgency in his posture, beer glasses abandoned

scenes.narration: Still nothing. The town slept on.
scenes.image_prompt: very wide shot of the burning farm tiny against black hills, the sleeping town's single road empty, no headlights

scenes.narration: The firehouse stood two and a half miles away.
scenes.image_prompt: a small brick firehouse at night, engine parked inside dark bay doors, lights off, deserted street

scenes.narration: Help arrived at eight in the morning.
scenes.image_prompt: a 1940s fire engine finally arriving in morning daylight before a smoking foundation — help, hours too late

scenes.narration: Seven hours. For a fire that finished in forty-five.
scenes.image_prompt: firemen at the edge of an ash pit with hats off and heads down, thin smoke rising past them

scenes.narration: What the ashes did not hold was stranger than the fire.
scenes.image_prompt: a long iron rake dragging slowly through fine grey ash, finding nothing, close low angle

scenes.narration: Five children. Not one bone.
scenes.image_prompt: five small empty wooden chairs around a kitchen table dusted in ash light, deserted

scenes.narration: The state fire marshal blamed faulty wiring.
scenes.image_prompt: the state fire marshal in a long coat writing on a clipboard beside charred wiring in the burned foundation, grey morning

scenes.narration: But the family had watched the lights burn bright through the fire.
scenes.image_prompt: the burning farmhouse with downstairs window lights glowing steady white while flames climb the roof

scenes.narration: Dead wiring does not keep the lights on.
scenes.image_prompt: a single glowing light bulb in a window frame with fire raging behind it, impossible calm light, faulty wiring myth

scenes.narration: Six days after Christmas Eve, the five were declared dead.
scenes.image_prompt: five paper certificates fanned across a dark desk under a banker's lamp, a wax seal, nobody around

scenes.narration: And on the coroner's jury that called it an accident.
scenes.image_prompt: a row of seated jurymen on a wooden bench in a small courtroom, faces in shadow

scenes.narration: sat the salesman who promised their house would burn.
scenes.image_prompt: one juryman turned away into shadow — the salesman's profile, hat on his knee, the bench lit around him

scenes.narration: His threat was two months old. Nobody asked him about it.
scenes.image_prompt: the farmhouse porch where the threat was spoken, winter light, door shut, a long shadow remembered across the boards

scenes.narration: George couldn't bear to look at the pit. So he buried it.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder driving a bulldozer, pushing dark earth into the black foundation hole, grey sky

scenes.narration: Five feet of fresh dirt, for a memorial garden.
scenes.image_prompt: a smooth rectangle of fresh dirt where a house once stood, one small pine at the center of the new memorial garden, deserted

scenes.narration: He didn't know he was sealing the evidence under it.
scenes.image_prompt: the buried garden plot under silent snowfall at dusk — the evidence sealed somewhere beneath, fence posts, nobody around

scenes.narration: Grief believes what it's told. Jennie tested it.
scenes.image_prompt: Jennie Sodder kneeling at a small backyard fire at night, feeding bones into the flames, face set hard

scenes.narration: She burned animal bones in the yard, again and again.
scenes.image_prompt: close-up of blackened animal bones lying whole among glowing embers in a backyard fire

scenes.narration: Every single time, the bones survived.
scenes.image_prompt: charred but intact bones laid in a row on a scorched tin sheet, backyard dark around them

scenes.narration: A crematorium worker finally told her why.
scenes.image_prompt: a crematorium worker in coveralls speaking beside an open furnace door, orange glow across his face

scenes.narration: Bones outlast two thousand degrees, for two full hours.
scenes.image_prompt: a furnace temperature gauge needle buried deep in the red, two thousand degrees of roaring light below

scenes.narration: Her house had burned for forty-five minutes.
scenes.image_prompt: Jennie Sodder standing at the garden fence, arms crossed, staring at the soil where her house burned

scenes.narration: Then the telephone man brought worse news.
scenes.image_prompt: a telephone lineman's spiked boots climbing a pole, coil of wire over his shoulder, grey sky

scenes.narration: The line never burned. It was sliced clean.
scenes.image_prompt: a cleanly cut telephone line end held up in a gloved hand, copper bright at the slice

scenes.narration: Cut by someone who climbed fourteen feet, in the dark, mid-fire.
scenes.image_prompt: a shadowed figure climbing high on the telephone pole against the dark night sky, a house burning small in the distance

scenes.narration: That same night, a man was seen hauling off their block and tackle.
scenes.image_prompt: a figure dragging heavy block and tackle away from a farm shed, edge-lit by distant firelight

scenes.narration: He admitted stealing it. He denied everything else.
scenes.image_prompt: a sullen man in a sheriff's office chair, hat in his hands, the tackle he admitted stealing on the desk beside him

scenes.narration: The pieces sat there for anyone to count.
scenes.image_prompt: evidence pieces laid out to count on a farmhouse table: cut wire, a toy, a photograph, an insurance paper, lamplight, nobody around

scenes.narration: Then the sightings began.
scenes.image_prompt: a two-lane mountain road disappearing into cold morning fog where the first sighting was reported, deserted

scenes.narration: A woman watching the fire swore she saw the children, in a passing car.
scenes.image_prompt: from a dark hillside: a 1940s sedan passing the burning farm, small pale children's faces in its rear window

scenes.narration: Fifty miles west, a tourist stop served five children breakfast the next morning.
scenes.image_prompt: five children at a roadside diner counter at dawn, a woman pouring cocoa, steam rising, wary quiet

scenes.narration: The owner was sure of the faces. Five of them. Quiet. Watched over.
scenes.image_prompt: close-up of five cocoa cups on the diner counter and small quiet hands around them, watched from off-frame, morning light

scenes.narration: A week later, a Charleston hotel worker recognized four of them.
scenes.image_prompt: a 1940s hotel lobby at night, four children seated stiff on a bench, two adults standing over them

scenes.narration: The adults with them spoke Italian, and shut her questions down.
scenes.image_prompt: a stern Italian-speaking man raising a flat hand to stop her questions, the children silent behind him, lobby shadows

scenes.narration: By morning, the whole party was gone.
scenes.image_prompt: the empty hotel bench in the morning light, one small forgotten mitten on the floor — everyone gone, deserted lobby

scenes.narration: George and Jennie begged the FBI to take the case.
scenes.image_prompt: George and Jennie Sodder at a post office counter mailing a thick envelope, winter coats, resolve

scenes.narration: J. Edgar Hoover replied: only if local officials invite us in.
scenes.image_prompt: an official government envelope held in Jennie Sodder's hands at a doorway, her thumb over the seal

scenes.narration: The local officials declined.
scenes.image_prompt: a town official's closed office door with frosted glass, hat rack beside it, empty corridor, silence

scenes.narration: So the family hired private detectives instead.
scenes.image_prompt: a private detective's desk: a map with pins, photographs, a full ashtray, one green lamp, nobody in the chair

scenes.narration: Then the fire chief made a confession of his own.
scenes.image_prompt: a dented fire chief's helmet resting on a wooden table in low light, nobody around

scenes.narration: He'd found a heart in the ashes, he said, and buried it in a box.
scenes.image_prompt: a small wooden box half-buried in ash inside a lantern circle, night

scenes.narration: The family made him dig it back up.
scenes.image_prompt: men with shovels digging the small box back up from the ash garden inside a ring of lantern light, long shadows

scenes.narration: A funeral director opened it. Beef liver. Untouched by fire.
scenes.image_prompt: an opened small box on a mortuary table, men recoiling in lamplight, clinical tiles behind

scenes.narration: A plant, meant to end the questions. It multiplied them.
scenes.image_prompt: the planted box with its lid ajar, alone on the table — a trick that raised more questions, long shadows

scenes.narration: In 1949, the family dug up the garden with a pathologist.
scenes.image_prompt: men excavating a neat trench in the memorial garden plot, wooden screens and trays beside them, overcast

scenes.narration: They sifted every shovelful of that dirt.
scenes.image_prompt: dark soil pouring through a wooden sifting screen, careful fingers picking through what remains

scenes.narration: Coins. A scorched dictionary. And four small vertebrae.
scenes.image_prompt: a metal specimen tray holding four small vertebrae beside a cleaning brush and burned coins, cold light

scenes.narration: The Smithsonian Institution examined the bones.
scenes.image_prompt: white-gloved museum hands examining the bones under a large magnifying lens in a laboratory

scenes.narration: One person. Sixteen to twenty-two years old. Never touched by fire.
scenes.image_prompt: extreme close-up through the magnifier: one pale unburned vertebra, clinical light

scenes.narration: Too old for the children, and most likely carried in with the fill dirt.
scenes.image_prompt: a dump truck tipping a load of fill dirt, flat grey recollection, deserted site

scenes.narration: Five children do not burn down into four borrowed bones.
scenes.image_prompt: the five oval child portraits rehung above a mantel in a new small house, lamplight, nobody around

scenes.narration: So in 1952, George built the billboard.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder hammering the frame of a giant roadside billboard beside a mountain highway, sleeves rolled

scenes.narration: Five painted faces. One question. A reward that grew to ten thousand dollars.
scenes.image_prompt: a huge roadside billboard bearing five painted child portraits and one painted question, mountain road curving past, 1950s cars small below

scenes.narration: It stood on Route sixteen for thirty-seven years.
scenes.image_prompt: the Route sixteen billboard standing through the years in deep winter, snow piled along its ledge, bare trees, empty road

scenes.narration: Drivers slowed for it. Nobody ever collected.
scenes.image_prompt: drivers' headlights sweeping across the billboard's five faces at night, nobody stopping to collect, empty two-lane road

scenes.narration: Then, in 1968, an envelope arrived with no return address.
scenes.image_prompt: a plain envelope lying on a doormat inside a shadowed hallway, light from the door seam

scenes.narration: Inside was a photograph of a man in his mid-twenties.
scenes.image_prompt: weathered hands holding a small black-and-white photograph of a young man, lamplight

scenes.narration: Louis's eyes. Louis's hair. Twenty-three years older.
scenes.image_prompt: the new photograph propped beside the old portrait of nine-year-old Louis, side by side under a lamp

scenes.narration: On the back, careful handwriting: Louis Sodder. I love brother Frankie.
scenes.image_prompt: the back of a photograph with faint soft-focus handwriting, held in trembling weathered fingers

scenes.narration: And a string of letters and numbers nobody ever decoded.
scenes.image_prompt: Jennie Sodder with a magnifying glass over the coded letters and numbers on the photograph, lamplight, midnight kitchen

scenes.narration: They hired a detective and sent him to Kentucky.
scenes.image_prompt: the hired detective boarding a Kentucky-bound bus with a suitcase at a rural depot, exhaust in cold air

scenes.narration: He took their money and was never heard from again.
scenes.image_prompt: the detective's abandoned office — phone off the hook, unopened mail under the door slot, the money gone, dust in the light

scenes.narration: The Sodders never published the details. What if it put Louis in danger?
scenes.image_prompt: George and Jennie Sodder by the fireplace at night, speaking low, the photograph between them

scenes.narration: They enlarged the photo and hung it over the fireplace.
scenes.image_prompt: a framed enlarged photo of the young man above a stone fireplace, two worn armchairs below, fire low

scenes.narration: So what happened that night? Two theories refuse to die.
scenes.image_prompt: the farmhouse evidence table seen from above — every strange piece of that night in one lamplit ring, nobody around

scenes.narration: The first points to extortion, the old country's mafia, and a man who wouldn't pay.
scenes.image_prompt: an old extortion letter sealed with a black handprint lying on a doormat, dread, low light

scenes.narration: Burn the house for the insult. Take the children for the debt.
scenes.image_prompt: five small children's silhouettes led through snow toward a dark sedan at night, doors open

scenes.narration: The second theory is stranger: the children were saved, not stolen.
scenes.image_prompt: a car door held open for children by an unseen adult, warm light inside the car, falling snow

scenes.narration: Spirited away to Italy, raised under other names, forbidden to write.
scenes.image_prompt: an Italian coastal village street at dusk, five small figures walking away up the hill, warm windows

scenes.narration: No theory has ever produced a body, or a grown child, come home.
scenes.image_prompt: the farmhouse gate standing open at dusk, the road beyond empty, nobody coming home, long shadows

scenes.narration: George died in 1969, still writing letters.
scenes.image_prompt: George Sodder asleep at a desk buried in letters, pen still in his fingers, lamp burned low

scenes.narration: Jennie wore black every day for the rest of her life.
scenes.image_prompt: Jennie Sodder in a black mourning dress at the garden fence, white-haired, mountains behind

scenes.narration: When she died in 1989, the billboard finally came down.
scenes.image_prompt: workmen lowering the weathered billboard face into a truck bed, grey sky, the five painted faces tilted

scenes.narration: Sylvia, the baby carried from the fire, kept asking until 2021.
scenes.image_prompt: Sylvia as an elderly woman by a window, holding the old family photograph from before the fire, morning light on her hands

scenes.narration: She never believed her brothers and sisters died that night.
scenes.image_prompt: close-up of an aged hand resting gently over the five brothers' and sisters' faces in an old photograph

scenes.narration: The fire took forty-five minutes.
scenes.image_prompt: the fire's ash pit once more, brief and dark, a single ember glowing in the grey

scenes.narration: The waiting took seventy-six years. It's still waiting.
scenes.image_prompt: the dark carnival exhibit pedestal again, spotlight on, the frame now empty, red curtain still

characters.name: George Sodder
characters.description: stocky Italian immigrant father in his fifties, weathered kind face, thick dark mustache, dark work trousers with suspenders over a rolled-sleeve flannel shirt, coal dust on his boots

characters.name: Jennie Sodder
characters.description: small careworn mother in her forties, dark hair pinned in a low bun, long plain 1940s housedress with a knitted shawl, in later years dressed all in black mourning clothes
characters.palette: faded navy, bone white, mourning black

fixes: video: empty global_style_suffix -> channel art direction
scene 67: emphasis markup -> eight in the morning
scene 75: added terminal period to narration
scene 85: emphasis markup -> two thousand degrees
scene 113: emphasis markup -> Never touched by fire
scene 126: emphasis markup -> never heard from again
warnings: long script (1347 words): one-take TTS can drift past ~5 min — spot-check the QA transcript extra carefully (chapter takes are the next fix if it drifts)

research.summary: On Christmas Eve 1945 the Sodder house in Fayetteville, WV burned in ~45 minutes; George, Jennie and four children escaped, five children (Maurice 14, Martha 12, Louis 9, Jennie 8, Betty 5) vanished — no remains were ever found. Phone line cut, ladder moved 75 ft, trucks disabled, fire dept arrived ~8am. Family maintained until 2021 the children survived.
facts: Fire: early hours Dec 25, 1945, Fayetteville WV; house destroyed in ~45 minutes
Missing: Maurice 14, Martha 12, Louis 9, Jennie 8, Betty 5; no remains found in ash
Oct 1945: insurance salesman threatened house would 'go up in smoke... children destroyed' over George's anti-Mussolini remarks; later sat on coroner's jury
Phone line was cut (14 ft climb), not burned; ladder found 75 ft down an embankment; both coal trucks would not start
Fire department 2.5 miles away arrived ~8 a.m. (phone-tree system, wartime manpower)
Fire marshal ruled faulty wiring though lights burned during fire; deaths certified Dec 30, 1945
Jennie's bone-burning experiments + crematorium testimony (2000F for 2 hours leaves bones)
Fire chief's buried box: contained beef liver untouched by fire
Aug 1949 excavation with pathologist Oscar B. Hunter: four lumbar vertebrae, one individual age 16-22, no fire exposure, likely from fill dirt (Smithsonian Institution analysis)
Sightings: children in passing car during fire; tourist stop breakfast ~50 mi west; Charleston hotel — four children with Italian-speaking adults
FBI declined without local invitation; locals declined
1952 billboard on Route 16, reward to $10,000, stood until Jennie's death 1989
1968 letter from Kentucky: photo of man ~mid-20s, back read 'Louis Sodder — I love brother Frankie'; PI sent to Kentucky vanished with the fee
George died 1969; Jennie wore black until her death 1989; Sylvia, last survivor, died 2021 still believing her siblings survived

titles: Five Children Went Upstairs. The Fire Left No Bones
Five children went upstairs on Christmas Eve. They never came down
Five Children Went Upstairs. The Fire Left No Bones: Every exhibit was real
seo.description:
Five children went upstairs on Christmas Eve. They never came down.
Rewind to ten p.m., December 24th, 1945.
Maurice. Martha. Louis. Little Jennie. Betty.

The Midnight Menagerie — Every exhibit was real.
